title: Heat Exchanger Fouling Loss Calculator
description: Calculates energy and production losses due to fouling in shell-and-tube heat exchangers, based on ISO 50001, Six Sigma DMAIC, and Lean manufacturing principles.

inputs.label: Design Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient × Area (UA_design)
inputs.businessContext: Clean design UA from manufacturer datasheet or initial commissioning test (ISO 50001:2018, Annex B).

label_i18n.en: Design Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient × Area (UA_design)

businessContext_i18n.en: Clean design UA from manufacturer datasheet or initial commissioning test (ISO 50001:2018, Annex B).

inputs.label: Actual Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient × Area (UA_actual)
inputs.businessContext: Current measured UA from process data (temperature, flow) using heat balance. Indicates fouling severity.

label_i18n.en: Actual Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient × Area (UA_actual)

businessContext_i18n.en: Current measured UA from process data (temperature, flow) using heat balance. Indicates fouling severity.

inputs.label: Hot Fluid Inlet Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of hot process stream entering the heat exchanger.

businessContext_i18n.en: Temperature of hot process stream entering the heat exchanger.

inputs.label: Cold Fluid Inlet Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of cooling or heating utility entering the exchanger.

businessContext_i18n.en: Temperature of cooling or heating utility entering the exchanger.

inputs.label: Hot Fluid Mass Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Mass flow rate of the hot process stream.

businessContext_i18n.en: Mass flow rate of the hot process stream.

inputs.label: Hot Fluid Specific Heat Capacity
inputs.businessContext: Average specific heat of hot fluid over operating temperature range.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average specific heat of hot fluid over operating temperature range.

inputs.label: Energy Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of thermal energy (steam, hot water, or electricity for pumping).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost of thermal energy (steam, hot water, or electricity for pumping).

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual operating hours of the heat exchanger.

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual operating hours of the heat exchanger.

inputs.label: Production Loss Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of production capacity lost due to reduced heat transfer (e.g., slower throughput).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of production capacity lost due to reduced heat transfer (e.g., slower throughput).

inputs.label: Revenue per Unit Product
inputs.businessContext: Revenue generated per kg of product processed through the exchanger.

businessContext_i18n.en: Revenue generated per kg of product processed through the exchanger.

inputs.label: Heat Exchanger Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of heat exchanger; affects fouling factors and cleaning intervals.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of heat exchanger; affects fouling factors and cleaning intervals.

inputs.label: Fouling Factor Known?
inputs.businessContext: If true, user provides measured fouling factor directly; otherwise calculated from UA ratio.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, user provides measured fouling factor directly; otherwise calculated from UA ratio.

inputs.label: Measured Fouling Factor (R_f)
inputs.businessContext: Directly measured fouling resistance from test or literature (TEMA standards).

businessContext_i18n.en: Directly measured fouling resistance from test or literature (TEMA standards).

rules.message: Actual UA cannot exceed design UA. Check measurement or input values.
Hot inlet temperature must be greater than cold inlet temperature for proper heat transfer.
If fouling factor is known, it must be a positive number.

thresholds.message: Fouling resistance exceeds 0.001 m²·K/kW – immediate cleaning recommended.
Annual energy loss cost exceeds $50,000 – investigate cleaning schedule optimization.
Production loss cost exceeds $100,000 – consider redundancy or advanced anti-fouling coatings.

formulas.label: Fouling Resistance (R_f)
formulas.description: Calculated from UA ratio or directly from user input.

formulas.label: Design Heat Duty (Q_design)
formulas.description: Maximum heat transfer capacity under clean conditions using LMTD method (simplified).

formulas.label: Actual Heat Duty (Q_actual)
formulas.description: Current heat transfer based on actual UA and LMTD approximation.

formulas.label: Energy Loss (ΔQ)
formulas.description: Reduction in heat duty due to fouling.

formulas.label: Annual Energy Loss Cost
formulas.description: Monetary value of lost thermal energy over a year.

formulas.label: Annual Production Loss Cost
formulas.description: Revenue lost due to reduced throughput caused by fouling.

formulas.label: Total Annual Fouling Loss
formulas.description: Primary result: sum of energy and production losses.

primary.label: Total Annual Fouling Loss
primary.description: Combined annual cost of energy loss and production loss due to fouling.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Increased Pumping Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional energy for pumps due to higher pressure drop across fouled exchanger.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Maintenance Downtime
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Lost production during cleaning or replacement.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Reduced Equipment Life
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Accelerated corrosion or erosion under fouling deposits.

suggestedActions.action: Schedule chemical cleaning or mechanical brushing within next 2 weeks.
Install online fouling monitoring system (e.g., thermal resistance sensors).
Evaluate anti-fouling coatings or surface modifications for future replacement.
Review process conditions (velocity, temperature) to minimize fouling rate.
